records.pText:
<align="center">●The Life and Times of the Grand Marshal’s Daughter (1)</align>
11/1/16 (Thu)
I was a little late getting to work today. When I arrived, the editor-in-chief called me into his office.
“Sorry, Ishelvdt, but that ‘Life and Times of the Grand Marshal’s Daughter’ story you put together—we’re gonna shelve it.”
I was too taken aback by the sudden news to do anything but ask why.

It turns out word had come into the duchy on the 25th of last month that the Sky Knights had been annihilated, and [PCF2] was now traveling with the wind vestal.
That was the reason, he said.
<align="center">* * *</align>
I hit Broth, Stock & Barrel that afternoon to drown myself in hot sake and cream stew with napa cabbage.

Broth, Stock & Barrel is a pub that specializes in simmering pots: stews, oden, pot-au-feu—you name it. The three-pot menu changes daily, and business for them in the City of Eternia was good. I’d been covering [PCF2], the grand marshal’s daughter, for eighteen years now. I’d write articles on her every year on her birthday, Father’s and Mother’s Day, and every four years on the Festival of the Reunion, and uncovered a number of (unpublished) scoops in the process.

“I even know the full story behind the Levy incident, blast it!”
I gripped my empty cup…
<align="center">* * *</align>
“Er, excuse us…”
I turned around to see two (gloomy-looking, and clearly) twin brothers standing behind me. They said they were technicians in the white magic research facility.

“We believe we have a different understanding on the Levy incident than you might.”
What?
Annoyed, I asked to interview them nonetheless, but they both made put-out faces.
“Our lunch break will be over soon. We’re terribly sorry.”
“But if you’ll meet us here three days from now…”

They said nothing more and left the pub.
<align="center">* * *</align>
Lunchtime on 11/4, three days later, the twins showed up at Broth, Stock & Barrel.
They’d come into the city once every three days to inspect the white magic cables, and this was their established lunch spot. They both ordered the A set: dried cod in clear broth. I had the B set: tripe simmered in rich miso.

First, they wanted to know what I knew of the Levy incident.
<align="center">* * *</align>
In the fall of EC 10, an urgent message arrived from Nother Levy, magistrate of the Levy territories in Eternia’s west.
“Inhabitants of our lands longing for the days of the Orthodoxy have holed up in the fortress! This is a revolt against the duchy!”

The grand marshal took the matter seriously and, coordinating with the swordmaster Kamiizumi, dispatched five hundred elite soldiers to the Levy territories to suppress the rebellion.
Hayreddin Barbarossa (29) commanded the force. His lieutenant was the ninja Konoe Kikyo (15). Accompanying the force, for whatever reason, were [PCF2] Lee (12) and Alternis Dim (17).

<align="center">* * *</align>
The rebellion was swiftly quashed, but not without incident: through their own carelessness, [PCF2] and Alternis, of all people, were taken hostage by the rebels.
Rumors around the port said that [PCF2] trying to have things her way had significantly hindered the operation, but due to her father the grand marshal’s influence, the precise details were never revealed.

<align="center">* * *</align>
That was the extent of my knowledge of the Levy incident. Naturally, not enough to make for an article.
“Mm-hmm, that’s what we thought.” The twins nodded in unison, and began to tell me what facts they knew.
“Firstly, it was not the rebellion that was swiftly quashed.”

Huh? My hand froze before I could even start taking notes. They grinned and continued on.
“It was Levy and his private army.”
<align="center">* * *</align>
From the outset, the marshal’s orders were not to suppress the rebellion by arms, but to meet them with negotiation. Even that bellicose-looking Captain Barbarossa was selected by Kamiizumi because he would be a dispassionate, unprovocative negotiator.

Even after [PCF2] was captured, the captain prevented his troops from acting rashly and poured all the more energy into the negotiations.
It was the magistrate Levy who grew impatient and sent his own soldiers into the fort to attack the rebels. The result was the rebel army admirably holding off Levy’s army at the fort, only for Levy’s to be “quashed” by Barbarossa’s from behind.

Why did Barbarossa, up until that time so focused on negotiations, suddenly attack Levy? To put it plainly, Levy’s misdeeds had been found out.
<align="center">* * *</align>
Levy’s misdeeds—these were a latent problem for the duchy as well.

Nine years earlier at the founding of the duchy, magisterial governance over its territories was put in place. As many of the territorial lords during the Orthodoxy’s time were installed in place as magistrates, it’s to be expected that many felt particularly possessive about the lands they were supposed to govern, Levy being one of those.
The cause of the rebellion was Levy being appointed to a new territory, and him raising heavy taxes on his old territory before he left.

Already he had long demanded a significant portion of his residents’ wealth, robbing their stores so they hadn’t even seeds left to sow in the spring. The revolt was inevitable.
Levy framed it as the people revolting against the duchy to return to the old ways of the Orthodoxy, and lured in five hundred soldiers to suppress the people.

“Nextly…” The one I assumed to be the elder twin began to cut in, but the younger stopped him.
“Brother, the time. We should get back to the cables.”
They said we could continue our conversation in three days, and both stood up in unison.
“Actually, [PCF2] let herself be caught by the rebels.”

Leaving me with a cliffhanger, the two left the pub. They also left the exact change for their lunches on the table.
Argh… I’d have to wait three whole days to hear the rest!?
I scribbled “INTERVIEW” in my schedule for 11/7.

<align="right">To Be Continued</align>

<align="center">●The Life and Times of the Grand Marshal’s Daughter (2)</align>
11/7/16 (Wed)
The twin technicians entered the pub a little after the lunch rush.
The A set was already sold out, so all three of us ordered the B set: simmered eggplant with minced meat.
They began to tell me what really happened with [PCF2] taken hostage during the Levy incident.
<align="center">* * *</align>

“[PCF2] wasn’t roaming outside of the encampment because she was bored, but rather she was investigating the territory and speaking to its inhabitants.”
Apparently she had taken to heart her master’s teachings, “Know thine enemy.”
<align="center">* * *</align>

Some years earlier, a flood had destroyed much of the territory’s arable land. Levy invested nothing into infrastructure for flood control or reclaiming the croplands, and instead taxed the people, taking their food and seeds. The revolt directly followed this.
[PCF2] understood and accepted the inhabitants’ claims, and suggested they take her as a hostage.

“Alright, Alternis, it’s sink or swim from here!”
[PCF2]’s words spurred the dark knight Alternis Dim to action.
Captain Barbarossa would not move. If the fort were attacked, it would be by Levy’s private army of a hundred. In that case…!
Alternis gathered the rebellion’s young men and women to remove the fort’s broken gate.

After that, he did not run any sort of drills, but simply sat in front of a plan of the fort drawn in the earth and stared at it.
<align="center">* * *</align>
One day, an old woman came to Alternis. “You’re a mighty bold commander to leave our front door open like that.”
Alternis answered her without taking his eyes off the floor plan.

“This fortress is old and full of holes in its walls. I can’t handle the enemy on my own if they’re spread out.”
The old woman laughed. “I see, you mean to make them second guess whether the fort is weakly defended, or if it’s a trap.”
Alternis continued looking at the plan.
“Not exactly. But while we’re at it, why not drop the disguise, Konoe Kikyo?”

With a “poof!” the old woman’s disguise vanished. “Youhaveafortfullofholesanduntrainedcivilians.Insteadofriskingincompletetraining,youthinkyourselfstrongenoughtoluretheenemytoasinglepointforyoutovanquish.Butwhatneedisthereforthegrandmarshal’sdaughtertocooperateasacaptive?ItmakesnosenseandhasCommanderBarbarossasoworriedhe—” Kikyo’s deluge of words without so much as a breath finally diverted Alternis’s line of sight. He handed her a letter.

“All the particulars are in here. See that it is delivered to the Lord Marshal and the swordmaster in all haste.”
In another puff of smoke, the ninja was gone.
<align="center">* * *</align>
Several days later, the impatient Levy began his attack. His soldiers rushed to the open gate, but the majority of them never made it that far.

Except for a single, straight path to the fort, pitfalls had been set up over a wide area. The remaining soldiers rushing ahead on the path were knocked aside by Alternis, one after the other.
<align="center">* * *</align>
Kikyo traveled at frightening speed, running from the encampment back to the seat of the duchy. With the new information in hand, the grand marshal immediately ordered the magistrate subdued, to which Barbarossa promptly complied.

<align="center">* * *</align>
Levy managed to escape Barbarossa’s encirclement with a handful of his close associates, but in the end, he lost his life at the hands of one of those associates.
The duchy felt it best this incident not be allowed to spread to other territories, and the details were suppressed.

<align="center">* * *</align>
There is an addendum to this incident, some days afterward. From the government’s perspective, even if the revolt was inevitable, the country could not permit its citizenry disregarding the rule of law without consequences. More than a few of the louder voices called for the rebellion’s leaders to bear the responsibility.
When [PCF2] returned home, she laid into the grand marshal agonizing over what to do.

“No way! Every single person who took part in the revolt should be put to hard labor!”
“What on earth is this child saying!?” the grand marshal must have thought, rendered speechless at his daughter’s harsh words.
Just as the grand marshal seemed ready to raise his voice in anger, [PCF2] cracked a mischievous sneer and followed up with this.

“Make every last one of them work to restore the Levy territories’ arable land!”
[PCF2] had heard from the old folks in the fort that all of the cropland destroyed by the flood could be reclaimed and made fruitful if they had hands to work them.
“B-but…” It wouldn’t be possible to accomplish such large-scale cultivation within the farming season, and ordering the people to do so would certainly trigger another revolt.

“Just send the whole army to lend them a hand next winter.”
[PCF2]’s advice was an aha moment for the grand marshal, who set off through the snow to again meet with his counselors.
<align="center">* * *</align>
The situation moved quickly after that. The government made an emergency purchase of surplus food from the villages of eastern Eternia and transported it to the Levy territories.

And the following winter after the harvest, reclamation works began in earnest. The grand marshal led his personal guard, and every division supplied a number of soldiers who ate, slept, and toiled alongside the territories’ citizens.
Needless to say [PCF2] and Alternis were among them as well.
The grand marshal, watching his daughter at work with the support of the people, apparently had this to say to the swordmaster at his side.

“She’s like a newly hatched chick, believing fully in the first thing she sees… I pray that whatever she sees from here on out be not injurious to the people.”
For the duchy, this reclamation of the Levy territories’ croplands may have been its last moment of respite.
<align="center">* * *</align>
“Huh? Why would we know all this about the Levy incident?” In response to my question, the twins smiled quietly at each other.

“We heard it aaaall from [PCF2] and Alternis.”
The white magic circulation hub where the twins worked was like a playground to [PCF2] and Alternis; or a site for exploration, as they called it.
I couldn’t help but smile at the thought of them sharing their exploits while gobbling up snacks over tea.

<align="center">* * *</align>
The twins left.
And with my project shelved, what a waste of a good story.
I had my now-cold eggplant cleared away and asked the barkeeper for a beer and whatever snacks he thought would go well with it.

<align="center">* * *</align>
After a few minutes, he brought me my beer along with something from the pub’s secret menu: ginger-simmered bear.
“Huh? Wait a second…!” This dish had come up when I was tracking down the truth to another incident!
I started flipping through the pages of an old notebook…

<align="right">To Be Continued</align>

<align="center">●The Life and Times of the Grand Marshal’s Daughter (3)</align>
It was past lunchtime at Broth, Stock & Barrel and diners were few and far between.
<align="center">* * *</align>
“Here, I knew it!” I had open a page in an old notebook with the date EC 13 on it.
There was a passage describing ginger-simmered bear from the secret menu in copious detail. It even had a smiley face drawn next to it.

<align="center">* * *</align>
EC 13… The year I interviewed her was a dizzying one. The Council of Six, Eternia’s highest governing body, was inaugurated, and the first through third divisions were all dispatched overseas. Both sides were deadlocked in Eisenberg’s civil war, and the fire vestal, Cassia Volg Oblige, was dead.

On this day, it seems I had again been here griping into my glass after being told off by the editor-in-chief.
“Blast him! He’s way too scared of upsetting the pols…”
And I had so many juicy stories lined up! “The Picnic Incident,” “The Abominable Snowman Situation,” “The Beating Not-Actually-A-Dojo-Challenger Affair,” and that one from EC 7…

“Do you ever shut up!?”
The abrupt abuse hurled at me brought me back to my senses.
“Incessantly whining about old news!”
The woman at the table behind me was turned around, glaring at me.
“And that last one you started to mention…”

She pointed at me with her mug. “You’re talking about ‘[PCF2]’s Psychotic Episode,’ aren’t you?”
I nodded. She drained her mug in one go and reseated herself next to me, prying into my notebook.
“Right, riiight, this one. And the way it’s written here, you obviously know nothing about it.”

<align="center">* * *</align>
The drunkard introduced herself as Holly Whyte (23), a white mage in the grand marshal’s personal guard.
(She may have been dressed in healing white, but everything about her screamed sadist.)
“Here, I’ll fix what you’ve got wrong, so give me your grasp of the episode, hm?”

When I hesitated, she shouted, “Hurry and spit it out already!” jutting her chin out at me.
<align="center">* * *</align>
“[PCF2]’s Psychotic Episode”
In EC 7, [PCF2] Lee, daughter of the grand marshal, attacked and injured more than a dozen sons of prominent Eternian families. The perpetrator was not held accountable, and people accused the grand marshal of nepotism behind his back.

“EC 7… Just when I was falling off the straight and narrow. Oh, how I remember it well.”
Holly gave her new mug a wistful look before draining it.
<align="center">* * *</align>
Holly’s family owned the famous drug manufacturer company Whyte Pharmaceutical.

Her parents died in the Great Plague.
Her grandfather made his fortune by integrating the waves flowing through the white magic cables into drug manufacturing.
She was apparently once involved with the man in charge of the cables, but had a falling-out with him and did not care for her grandfather’s use of the technology, and afterward began to run with an unsavory crowd.

“Enough about me!”
Her brows knitted together in displeasure, but she turned cheerily talkative again after I ordered her another round.
“You know of the dark knight Alternis Dim, right?”
Of course I did. A member of the Council of Six at the fresh, young age of twenty this year.

He was recently given his own personal airship, the Black Knight, and spent his days busily flying here and there.
Holly snorted at my shallow knowledge and started talking about his early days.
<align="center">* * *</align>
Ten years ago…EC 3.
The templar took in an orphan from the slums of Florem. That was Alternis Dim (10).

Abandoned by his mother, he just barely managed to survive. It must have been an ordeal.
When the templar was visiting Florem to inspect it, Alternis tried to swipe his purse and instead got knocked a good one.
I don’t know what transpired between those two, but Alternis took the hand the templar extended and found himself a loving home.

“When he first arrived in the Lee household, he was a bizarre kid…”
Holly was snatching bites of my bear while she enjoyed her beer.
<align="center">* * *</align>
Maybe he was doing it since his time as a street urchin, but he always covered his face with a thin black cloth. Didn’t want to show anyone his expression. Didn’t want to talk to anyone. He was still like that the first few years after he arrived. It was an ordeal.

[PCF2] was five years younger, but she’d still talk to him, read him picture books like she was his big sister. Makes you laugh, huh?
<align="center">* * *</align>
Four years after Alternis came to the Lees would be EC 7. [PCF2] (9), Alternis (14), and Holly (17).
Little by little, he started to open up at home.

He came in as a skinny waif, but in addition to a home, the templar gave him sword lessons, too.
At a glance, you’d expect anyone to want the life he got. But his personality, always wearing that weird veil and not speaking a word, made him the target of spiteful envy.
<align="center">* * *</align>
One day in a back alley, he stopped someone up to a little no good.

Alternis was brusque about it and impossibly intimidating, forcing the kid to stop. And that juvenile delinquent was the son of a prominent family. He surrounded Alternis with fourteen of his friends.
With the training Alternis has received from the templar, this gang would be no match for him. But looking closely, he could see a number of prominent sons among them.

He put his hands behind his back and gritted his teeth. He didn’t want his actions to cause any trouble for the Lee family.
<align="center">* * *</align>
A few hours later he stumbled home, covered in cuts and bruises. His black veil was stolen as a prize.

The templar had just left to attend to official duties. Mahzer frantically tended Alternis’s wounds.
Nine-year-old [PCF2] went to her room to retrieve her (third) “perfect stick,” and ran out of the house.
<align="center">* * *</align>
“Sounds just like her, right?”
Holly held out her nearly empty mug, smirking.

<align="right">To Be Continued</align>

<align="center">●The Life and Times of the Grand Marshal’s Daughter (4)</align>
[PCF2]’s psychotic episode in the year of EC 7 was actually her exacting revenge!
I scribbled notes without looking at what I was writing. Would I be able to read these later? That’s what I worried, but the only time Holly wasn’t talking was if her mouth was full of beer—I didn’t have time to check.

[PCF2] and her Perfect Stick III gathered the boys who attacked Alternis into an alley.
At first the boys made light of the little girl, but after she knocked out the first two, they started to see things differently.
By the fourth boy, Perfect Stick III broke in two. From the fifth onward, it was circles of punches, lightning kicks, headbutts, bites—no holds barred. Before she knew it, all fifteen boys lay in the dust.

Naturally every one of them ran home crying to their parents. That fifteen boys ganged up on a nine-year-old girl was bad enough, but that she wiped the floor with them was the finishing touch on their wall of shame. There would be no end to the embarrassment should word get out. The parents went to the templar begging that no more fuss be made of the issue. It avoided becoming a public incident (though Braev was furious at them). As for [PCF2], the templar praised her chivalrous spirit.

<align="center">* * *</align>
Holly emptied her seventh mug and lined it up on the table with the others. I looked to the barkeeper and he was already bringing the next round.
<align="center">* * *</align>
“When [PCF2] came home, she was covered all over in scrapes.” The white mage had a gratified look on her face.

“Alternis! I got it back for ya!”
In her hand was his black veil.
“This is important to you, isn’t it?”
Her forehead was covered in lumps and one of her teeth was chipped.
Alternis was still reticent then, but as he administered first aid, he made a rare exception.

“[PCF2], I promise…I promise that I will protect you! And this whole family, as long as I breathe.”
“As long as you breathe?”
“Forever, I mean.”
“Hah! It’s a deal!”
Who knows if she actually appreciated what he meant at that time, but they spent the two hours after that reading “Barbecues of the World” together.

<align="center">* * *</align>
“How do you know that much about Alternis? He couldn’t have told you himself.”
Holly looked at me with a bemused, “didn’t I tell you already?” sort of expression.
“You know of the knight Argent Heinkel, right?”

Of course I did. He was one of the pillars of the uprising, whom the templar trusts thoroughly and who now serves as the captain of the templar’s guard.
“Right. My boss.”
She shoveled the last bite of my bear into her mouth and grunted at the bartender for another. From the kitchen he shrugged his shoulders as if to say, “I give up.”

The dark arts Alternis learned to survive were self taught. Being put in his place by the templar, he recognized his dark arts were weak and resolved to truly study the sword.
My boss is the one who got him out of his self-taught bad habits and pounded proper sword technique into him. Studying under the captain, naturally he spent time with us other guard members, and he started to speak to us.

<align="center">* * *</align>
“Now what do you think that black cloth Alternis put on his head was?”
Holly fumbled a bit before continuing.
That cloth is the one keepsake he has from his mother.
The birth mother who abandoned him, that is.

You can’t tell now if it was a scarf or a cape or what. Now it’s just a rag.
For him, you could say it serves as the source of his resentment.
That’s what [PCF2] went and took back, saying it was important to him. Going up against fifteen older boys and getting pretty banged up in the process. Something else, huh?

“Well, she’s still just a snot-nosed brat in my book,” Holly said with a wry smile.
<align="center">* * *</align>
In time, Alternis would grow and wrap himself in jet-black armor. According to Holly, he kept that cloth close to him, under his armor.

“I’ve seen him several times. He certainly cuts an imposing figure.” I don’t expect he’d grant any request for an interview.
At my tiny whimper, Holly almost spit her beer out.
“Hahaha! What are you whining about? You know he was sitting at the counter right there eating till just a while ago, right?”

What!? I started looking all around fecklessly.
“C’mon, the guy with a pompadour. Blue jacket with white fur? You really don’t remember? You should learn to be more attentive.”
“He usually keeps his helmet on even at home,” she said. “If he was in street clothes, he might’ve been investigating something.” Holly glanced toward the door.

“Aha, finally my company’s here. Well, I’ll be going. Thanks for the drinks!”
In the doorway waited a small mountain of a man. The two of them disappeared into the twilight.
Looking back at my table, on it was the check with all its addendums. The barkeeper winked at me from afar. I could feel the muscles in my face stiffen.

<align="center">* * *</align>
12/7/16 (Fri)
I find myself in Broth, Stock & Barrel again.
My “Life and Times of the Grand Marshal’s Daughter” was shelved after all. Lately I’m being put on fluff pieces—food reviews, pet specials—as inconsequential as they get.

<align="center">* * *</align>
This morning I spotted Alternis’s Black Knight flying off to the west.
The government’s engaged in a full-blown cover-up; not a single paper covered news of the wind vestal and her party awakening the Wind Crystal.
And a little before that, word came in that Holly and the Sky Knights in Caldisla were killed in action.

[PCF2] seems to be traveling with the wind vestal, who is wanted by the duchy. At the same time, Alternis has his hands full flying around as a member of the Council of Six. What sort of reunion is in store for these two who grew up like siblings?
<align="center">* * *</align>
I placed my cup of hot sake on the table a little too hard.

That’s right! This story’s not shelved!
“It’s just resting, like bear meat. In time, it’ll be aged perfectly for simmering in ginger and soy sauce!” That’s what I shouted before I wrote “The Life and Times of the Grand Marshal’s Daughter” on the fat manila envelope and sealed it.
Reporting for the Eternia Journal
<align="right">Stor Ishelvdt</align>
